rawMeaning: The SPQR Study Guides series is designed to make it easy for students of all levels to dip their toes into antiquity. It includes 20 volumes of classical books broken down by chapter, with each Latin chapter followed by its equivalent English chapter. It also gives you the running vocabulary for each chapter as you read, in case you need a little help or if you’re revising the text for an exam.

This Latin frequency dictionary is designed to help students prioritise their language learning by highlighting the most common words they are likely to find. It was produced by assembling 1.5 million words from classical authors such as Caesar, Catullus, Horace, Martial, Ovid, Suetonius and Virgil, parsing each word to find the root meaning, then translating that meaning into English.

Words used at least 50-99 times abscedo, abscedere, abscessi, abscessus (v, 3rd) withdraw, depart, retire; go / pass off / away; desist; recede (coasts); slough. 99 cubo, cubare, cubui, cubitus (v, 1st) lie (down / asleep); recline, incline; lie / be in bed, rest / sleep; be sick / dead. 99
